references.text: [He00] Heath-Brown, D. R., Arithmetic applications of {K}loosterman sums. Nieuw Arch. Wiskd. (5) (2000), 380--384.

remarks.text:
Heilbronn (unpublished) proved this for $c$ sufficiently close to $1$. Heath-Brown \cite{He00} used Kloosterman sums to prove this for all $c>3/4$. 

This is discussed in this MathOverflow question.

statement.text: Is it true that, for any $c>1/2$, if $p$ is a sufficiently large prime then, for any $n\geq 0$, there exist $a,b\in(n,n+p^c)$ such that $ab\equiv 1\pmod{p}$?
